items.text: Yeah, I’m over here in Southeast Arizona, and we got hard water and it is a pain to clean😅

Bar soap in our house never got used right away. Ma unpackaged every bar and air dried them for weeks.If you dry the bars completely, they last days longer! They dont mush up! You can feel the difference! Much harderCompanies dont dry them much. They set you up to fail so you buy more when they melt away fast.Ma would buy 12 bars at a time, unbox them, put them in a bowl high on the shelf. While drying, they also freshened the air!Once that batch was dried totally, they got stacked up to be used next. Then the next batch got put up and so on.Hope this helps! God bless! 🙏

I’m fortunate in that I do not have hard water. I had excellent quality well water filtered through the sandy soil if Long Island’s pine barrens. I never buy a  cabinet’s worth of cleaning products. I use vinegar, baking soda, hydrogen peroxide, for cleaning agents.
I think those companies are the same ones who do painful experiments on animals. I do not buy from those companies and appreciate so much you teaching about cruelty free ways.

Esther, thank you for your videos. At 75 years old, I am learning so much from you and the Amish Wisdom videos. I have used vinegar for cleaning my electric kettle, in place of fabric softener in the washing machine, removing hard water around the base of faucets and showerheads for 20 years here in central France. I have also (since 1980) removed tarnish from solid silver flatware using aluminum foil and baking soda in warm (not hot) water, but not as well as your version of aluminum foil, hot water, and washing soda…because my pieces were not individually touching the foil…two simple changes will do the trick the next time.
